title: Cystanthe gunnii
intro: Cystanthe gunnii, also known as Gunn's lily or mountain lilyturf, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand and Australia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cystanthe gunnii include flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cystanthe gunnii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Cystanthe gunnii is classified within the plant family Marantaceae. This family is commonly known as the prayer plant family. The specific classification of Cystanthe gunnii can be found within the genus Cystanthe.
